data.title: What to expect from Costco in 2025
data.description:
Costco (NASDAQ:COST) heads into the new year on a high note after a +45% year-to-date rally and a string of 8 consecutive quarterly profit beats. The retailer also has incremental tailwinds as membership fee income increases start to anniversary in and the crackdown on entering stores without a valid membership card factors in.

Gordon Haskett analyst Chuck Grom thinks the biggest catalyst of all for Costco (COST) will be a stock split. "With the stock recently hitting all-time highs and touching four digits... the sticker shock value of Costco's share price is starting to match the valuation that is head and shoulders above most of our coverage universe," he noted. Grom reminded investors that stock splits do not affect the valuation of a company, but can be beneficial as the lower individual share price makes the company's stock appear more accessible to a wider pool of investors, including warehouse-level associates.

Another positive of Costco (COST) splitting its stock is that it ensures all associates can purchase shares. Grom also pointed out that the two retail giants that have split their stock in the past year, Walmart (WMT) and Williams-Sonoma (WSM) are up ~80% and ~90% year-to-date, respectively.

Costco (COST) heads into the new year, with bearish-leaning analysts thinking the share price does not line up with the revenue and EPS growth rates.

The Issaquah, Washington company fired off its last stock split in January 2000. At the time of that split, Costco's (COST) stock was trading for a pre-split price of about $100. Shares of COST edged 0.12% higher in early trading on Thursday to $965.88.

data.title: Coach to lead Tapestry's growth with Capri left in the dust - analyst
data.description:
With the Capri (CPRI) drama behind it and the company now able to focus on its flagship brand, Wall Street can prioritize Tapestry (NYSE:TPR) as a quality name given improving sales growth, expanding margins, and increased share buybacks. The upbeat outlook and optimistic forecast for Coach sales earns Tapestry (TRP) an upgrade at Jefferies to Buy from Hold and corresponding 60% hike in the target price to $80.

Shares are up 2% at Thursday’s open, on track to linger near its 12-year-high.

“Our buy rating [on Tapestry] is based on our above consensus outlook for FY26 given improving Coach sales growth, gross margin upside, and buybacks, in addition to multiple expansion given improved growth and stability with limited wholesale exposure,” Jefferies analyst Ashley Helgans said in her research note.

Coach is expected to drive growth at Tapestry (TPR) as changes at Kate Spade and Stuart Weitzman are underway, but taking more time and not as important to the overall company. Meanwhile, Coach is in the early stages of its average unit retail (“AUR”) journey, Helgans says, with brand heat not slowing down. She forecasts low-to-mid-single-digit sales growth for Coach based on the current trajectory, driving Tapestry (TPR) sales up 2-3% and gross margin by +80-100 basis points.

The outlook for the company also considers $2B in share buybacks, which can be viewed as management’s “vote of confidence” in the stock. Jefferies also updated its net debt and interest expense for the company and assumes $800M of run-rate repo afterward. This leads to an increase in EPS growth expectations to ~13% CAGR over the next two years. For FY26, Helgans models EPS at $5.51 versus the $5.05 consensus.

Despite Helgans bullish thesis for Tapestry (TPR), investors should proceed cautiously as the stock’s impressive run since August has resulted in a 9% short interest to float, making it one of the more shorted stocks within the S&P 500 according to Seeking Alpha analytics. This compares to a short interest of 8% for Capri (CPRI) and 5.3% for Ralph Lauren (RL).

data.title: Nuwellis regains compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements
data.description:
• Nuwellis (NASDAQ:NUWE) received formal written notice from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement, as well as Nasdaq’s stockholders’ equity requirement.
  • Nestor Jaramillo, President and CEO of Nuwellis, said: "NUWE raised of $5.1M in gross proceeds from warrant exercises and through recent warrant inducement transaction in November. In addition, we implemented a reverse stock split of 1:35 on June 26, 2024."
  • Nuwellis will be subject to a mandatory panel monitor for a year from the date the letter was received, December 17, 2024.
  • NUWE shares were down 6.5% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Southwest Airlines is cleared in FAA safety review of incidents
data.description:
The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration did not identify any significant safety issues with Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE:LUV) in a safety review of Southwest Airlines that was opened in July and is now completed.

The review was prompted by a series of high-profile incidents that raised serious concerns. The FAA began the safety review after a Southwest (LUV) plane in July flew at a very low altitude over Florida. That incident was quickly followed by several others that went viral on social media circles.

For its part, Southwest Airlines (LUV) said it appreciated the opportunity to engage with the FAA as part of the mutual dedication to safety.

Looking ahead, Southwest Airlines (LUV) has several significant plans for 2025. The carrier will launch 8 new domestic routes in August 2025, including flights between Austin and San Francisco, Nashville and Louisville, and Phoenix and Santa Barbara. The airline will also introduce red-eye flights in February 2025, connecting various cities such as Honolulu to Los Angeles and Seattle to Baltimore.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) also plans to offer premium seats with extra legroom in approximately one-third of its seats, starting in the second half of 2025. The airline will begin selling assigned seats in the second half of 2025, with the first flights operating under this new model in early 2026. Finally, Southwest (LUV) intends to partner with international carriers, starting with Icelandair in 2025.

Shares of LUV were up 0.6% in early trading on Thursday. The airline stock is up about 13% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Omeros jumps as lead drug succeeds in pivotal trial for blood transplant patients
data.description:
Shares of Omeros Corporation (NASDAQ:OMER) climbed ~55% in the morning hours on Thursday after the company said its lead candidate, narsoplimab, met the primary endpoint in a pivotal trial for stem cell transplant patients.

The trial OMS721-TMA-001 evaluated the potential of narsoplimab against hematopoietic stem cell transplant-associated thrombotic microangiopathy (TA-TMA), a complication of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT).

Citing a primary data analysis agreed with the FDA, Omeros (NASDAQ:OMER) said patients on narsoplimab lived longer (overall survival) with a clinically meaningful and statistically significant effect compared to those not on narsoplimab.

The analysis compared data from 28 HSCT patients on the company's previously completed OMS721-TMA-001 trial for narsoplimab with more than 100 similarly ill HSCT patients who didn’t receive the drug according to an external registry.

Given the outcome, OMER said it would resubmit a Biologics License Application in the U.S. for narsoplimab as soon as possible to position the drug as the first FDA-approved treatment option for TA-TMA. An EU marketing authorization application for expected in Q2 2025.

data.title: Revance Therapeutics ticks higher as holder says Crown deal undervalues company
data.description:
Revance Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RVNC) edged higher by 0.3% after holder Teoxane said Crown Laboratories $3.10 a share deal undervalues the company.

Teoxane has a 6.2% stake in Revance (RVNC), according to a 13D filing on Thursday.

"The Reporting Person believes that the Proposed Transaction undervalues the Issuer and that the consummation of the Proposed Transaction is not in the best interests of the Issuer or its stockholders," according to the filing.

The filing comes after Revance (RVNC) shares tumbled 21% last Monday after the company agreed to a revised deal with Crown Laboratories, with the takeover price cut by more than half. Revance holders will receive $3.10 a share in a revised takeover agreement, down from the original price of $6.66.

The parties entered into the amended and restated merger agreement after Revance (RVNC) received notice from Teoxane alleging breach by Revance of its exclusive distribution agreement with Teoxane.

data.title: U.S. Leading Indicator Index surprises to upside in November
data.description:
• November U.S. Leading Indicators: +0.3% M/M to 99.7 vs. -0.1% consensus and -0.4% prior.
  • Coincident Economic Index: +0.1% to 113.0 vs. 112.9 in October (revised from 112.8).
  • Lagging Economic Index: +0.3% to 118.8 vs. 118.5 prior (revised from 118.7).

data.title: Existing home sales climb 4.8% in November, exceeding consensus
data.description:
U.S. existing home sales rose 4.8% M/M in November to 4.15M, topping the 4.09M consensus and October's 3.96M (unrevised), the National Association of Realtors said in a report released on Thursday.

“Home sales momentum is building,” said NAR Chief Economist Lawrence Yun. “More buyers have entered the market as the economy continues to add jobs, housing inventory grows compared to a year ago, and consumers get used to a new normal of mortgage rates between 6% and 7%.”

data.title: NICE downgraded due to CEO transition, overestimated cloud growth: Jefferies
data.description:
NICE (NASDAQ:NICE) was downgraded to a Hold from a Buy rating from Jefferies due to several reasons, including an upcoming CEO transition, overestimated cloud growth in 2025 and increasing competitive pressure.

Jefferies also reduced its price target on the stock to $200 from $215. Shares were down about 2% during early Thursday market action.

In August, the NICE board of directors appointed Scott Russell as the new CEO, effective Jan. 1, 2025. For the last 14 years, Russell was at SAP (SAP), most recently as a member of the Executive Board of the Company and Global Chief Revenue Officer.

Although Jefferies noted it has received positive feedback from Russell's term at SAP, the departure of long-term CEO Barak Eilam could create risks.

"For the past few Q's, we have been highlighting that consensus estimates for Cloud rev growth were too high for 2H24 and 2025," said Jefferies analysts Samad Samana, in a Thursday note. "Despite mgmt lowering the organic forecast for 2024 (+16.5% at the midpoint) on the 3Q24 call, consensus expectations for 2025 did not reset enough. We are forecasting growth of 15% for 2025, well-below consensus of 16.6%."

Jefferies also highlighted that the Contact Center as a Service market, or CCaaS, which provides a cloud-based software solution, has grown more competitive.

"Beyond the CCaaS incumbents, large cap software vendors and the hyperscalers have all released externally-facing GenAI tools (intelligent virtual agents and chatbots) and internally facing GenAI tools," Samana added.

Jefferies notes that Saleforce's (CRM) Agentforce provides a prime example of the tough competition in this space.

data.title: US government shutdown unlikely to be protracted, Goldman says
data.description:
The risk of a US government shutdown for Friday has risen after President-elect Donald Trump announced his opposition to the bill while demanding an increase in the debt limit by a protracted shutdown seems unlikely, according to Goldman Sachs.

The firm said in a note late Wednesday that it doesn’t anticipate a near-term debt limit increase. The matter hadn’t been under discussion, it added.

“We estimate that a shutdown would reduce GDP growth in the quarter by 0.15 [percentage point] for each week it lasted and would boost GDP growth by the same amount in the quarter following government reopening,” Chief Political Economist Alec Phillips said.

Congressional leaders on Tuesday released a continuing resolution to fund the government until March 14. The measure included more than $100B in disaster and agricultural aid, among the provisions.

Trump said he backed extending the funding of the government, including the bill’s aid for disaster relief and agriculture. It could be possible that a reworked spending bill could be passed before Friday’s deadline, Goldman said.

Meanwhile, congressional Republicans could also try to pass a “clean” spending extension to skirt a shutdown, but that would delay items like disaster relief until early next year, the note said.

Goldman said the odds don’t seem to favor a near-term debt limit increase, which expires Jan. 2. However, there doesn’t seem to be a lot of pressure to address the matter now since the Treasury will be able to continue to borrow under the debt limit until Q3.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
